title: cassine capensis
intro: Cassine capensis, also known as wild olive or buchu, is a small shrub native to South Africa and is a member of the Rutaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its essential oils and other compounds. The health benefits of Cassine capensis include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as relieving symptoms of anxiety and stress. Cassine capensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in South Africa.
